data.title: STOCKS | Alibaba and JD.com Shares Rise in Pre-Market Trading
data.description: Alibaba and JD.com experienced notable gains in pre-market trading, with Alibaba's shares increasing by 4.4% and JD.com's shares rising by 4.6%. According to Jin10, these movements come amid broader market trends and investor sentiment shifts. The rise in stock prices reflects positive market reactions and potential investor confidence in these major Chinese tech companies. The pre-market performance of these stocks may influence trading dynamics as the market opens.

data.title: Aha Group Executives Sentenced for Crypto Scam Involving 46.8 Billion Won
data.description: The Changwon branch of the Busan High Court has sentenced Aha Group executives to 12-year and 9-year prison terms for their involvement in a cryptocurrency investment scam. According to NS3.AI, the scam raised approximately 46.8 billion won from 2,138 victims. The court found that the group had organized an unregistered multi-level marketing network starting in 2016. The appeals court slightly reduced the sentences from the first trial but upheld the convictions and emphasized the severity of the fraud.

data.title: Plume's Salman Banaei to Testify on Tokenized Securities Before U.S. House Committee
data.description: Salman Banaei, head of legal at the regulated real-world asset blockchain project Plume, is set to testify today at 2:00 p.m. UTC before the U.S. House Financial Services Committee. According to NS3.AI, Banaei is anticipated to highlight concerns regarding the migration of tokenization market infrastructure offshore due to policy uncertainty in the United States. He is expected to advocate for a more proactive regulatory approach to address these challenges.

data.title: China Criticizes U.S. Ban on Foreign-Made Consumer Routers
data.description: On March 25, according to Jin10, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian addressed a routine press conference. During the session, a reporter inquired about the U.S. Federal Communications Commission's decision to ban the import of all foreign-made consumer-grade routers. Lin Jian expressed China's consistent opposition to the U.S.'s broadening of national security concepts and its discriminatory actions against foreign companies. He emphasized that the U.S. should provide a fair, transparent, and non-discriminatory business environment for companies from all countries.

data.title: Deutsche Bank: The petrodollar system was already under pressure before the outbreak of war.
data.description: The petrodollar system is underpinned by three core elements: U.S. demand for oil, oil pricing in dollars, and the security relationship between the Gulf region and Washington. All three are currently under pressure. First, the U.S.'s new status as a net energy exporter means it is no longer heavily reliant on Middle Eastern oil supplies. Second, the dollar-denominated oil market was already deteriorating before the war. Many countries have been striving for years to price energy trade in their own currencies, making some progress, albeit limited. Third—and most seriously—the war has plunged U.S. protectionist policies and the Gulf region's trust in these policies into uncertainty. According to a report released Tuesday by Deutsche Bank on the current state of the petrodollar system, strategist Marika Sahdewa argues that the petrodollar system was already under pressure before the war: most Middle Eastern oil now flows to Asia; sanctioned Russian and Iranian oil is traded in non-dollar currencies; and Saudi Arabia is not only localizing its defense industry but also experimenting with non-dollar currencies for oil payments. (Jinshi)

data.title: Brent crude oil fell below $100 as diplomatic efforts eased concerns about escalating tensions in the Middle East.
data.description: Brent crude oil prices fell below $100 a barrel after the U.S. presented Iran with a 15-point plan aimed at ending the war, and reports indicated that mediators were pushing for a meeting between the U.S. and Iran later this week. "Wars rarely need to end before the market bottoms out," said Charu Chanana, an analyst at Saxo Bank. "Investors tend to act once the likelihood of further escalation begins to decline." Nevertheless, attacks by both sides continue, and the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed, with Iran stating that only "non-hostile" vessels can pass through the waterway. (Jinshi)

data.title: Hong Kong Prioritizes Strong IPO Supply, Says Financial Secretary
data.description: Hong Kong's Financial Secretary, Paul Chan, emphasized the importance of maintaining a robust supply of initial public offerings (IPOs) for the region's financial market. Bloomberg posted on X that Chan highlighted the strategic focus on IPOs as a key component of Hong Kong's economic agenda. He noted that a steady stream of IPOs is crucial for sustaining the city's status as a leading global financial hub. Chan's remarks underscore the government's commitment to fostering a favorable environment for companies seeking to go public, thereby enhancing market liquidity and attracting international investors. The financial secretary's comments come amid a competitive global landscape where other financial centers are also vying for IPO activity. Hong Kong aims to leverage its unique position and regulatory framework to remain a preferred destination for companies looking to list their shares.

data.title: Musk: X will suspend its planned "increase the weighting of creators' location in terms of exposure".
data.description: Musk stated that the planned "increased weighting of X creators' regions" is paused and will be further considered. Previously, Nikita Bier, head of X products, disclosed that the X platform would adjust its creator revenue-sharing incentive mechanism starting this Thursday (March 26), focusing on increasing the weighting of exposure from the creator's region. This adjustment aims to encourage content that resonates with users in the creator's home country, neighboring countries, and those speaking the same language, while reducing the incentive for creators to specifically optimize for traffic from US or Japanese accounts, thereby promoting a more diverse content ecosystem on the platform.

data.title: Oil Dollar System Faces Pressure Amid Shifts in Global Energy Trade
data.description:
The oil dollar system, traditionally supported by U.S. oil demand, dollar-denominated oil pricing, and security ties between the Gulf region and Washington, is currently under strain. According to Jin10, the United States' new status as a net energy exporter means it is less reliant on Middle Eastern oil supplies. Additionally, the practice of pricing oil in dollars has been deteriorating even before the outbreak of war, with many countries striving to conduct energy trade in their own currencies, albeit with limited success.

The most significant challenge arises from the war, which has cast doubt on the U.S. protective policy and the Gulf region's trust in it. A report released by Deutsche Bank on Tuesday highlights that the oil dollar system was already under pressure before the war. Currently, most Middle Eastern oil is directed towards Asia, while sanctioned Russian and Iranian oil is traded in non-dollar currencies. Furthermore, Saudi Arabia is localizing its defense industry and exploring non-dollar currencies for oil transactions.

data.title: Senegal's Use of Derivative Instruments in Debt Raising Sparks Debate
data.description: Senegal has utilized complex derivative instruments to raise debt, a move that has sparked discussions about transparency. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the country's finance ministry has defended the process as transparent. The use of such financial tools has raised questions among observers regarding the clarity and openness of the transactions involved. Despite the concerns, Senegal's finance ministry maintains that the procedures adhered to necessary transparency standards. The debate continues as stakeholders assess the implications of using derivative instruments in national debt management.

data.title: Iraq Defense Ministry: Airstrike in Anbar Province Kills 7 Militants, Injures 13
data.description: According to Jin10, the Iraqi Defense Ministry reported that an airstrike in Anbar Province resulted in the deaths of seven militants and injuries to thirteen others. The incident highlights ongoing security challenges in the region. Further details about the operation or the identities of those involved were not disclosed.

data.title: OKX released its 41st Proof-of-Response (PoR), with the total value of BTC, ETH, USDT, and USDC reaching $25.6 billion.
data.description: According to official news, OKX has officially released its 41st Proof of Reserves (PoR). This release shows that the reserve ratios for all 22 major cryptocurrencies disclosed by OKX have reached 100%. Specifically, the reserve ratios for BTC, ETH, USDT, and USDC are 106%, 107%, 109%, and 102%, respectively. Based on the prices on the verification date, the total value of the four reserve assets reaches $25,658,384,955. OKX is reportedly the first cryptocurrency exchange in the industry to achieve over three consecutive years of PoR releases. During this period, OKX has consistently released PoRs monthly, increasing the number of disclosed cryptocurrencies from 3 to 22, and continuously upgrading its PoR system through technologies such as Merkle trees, full-view Merkle trees, and zk-STARK, committed to increasing transparency of user assets.

data.title: Stellar's XLM Surges 14% in Two Days, Approaching Key Resistance Level
data.description: Stellar's XLM has experienced a notable increase of approximately 14% over the past two days, reaching the 61.8% Fibonacci retracement level at $0.1776. According to NS3.AI, the current price stands at $0.1777 on the 12-hour chart. Analysts suggest that surpassing the $0.1776 mark could pave the way for a rise to $0.2010. Conversely, a rejection at this level might lead to a dense long liquidation zone around $0.1630, with $4.30 million in leverage potentially at risk.

data.title: Indian Insurers Request Regulatory Easing on State Bond Capital Requirements
data.description: Indian insurers have approached their regulatory body to reconsider a proposal mandating them to allocate capital when purchasing state bonds. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the insurers' concerns that the current proposal could impact their investment strategies and financial flexibility. The insurers argue that the requirement to set aside capital for state bond purchases may lead to reduced investment in these securities, potentially affecting the broader bond market. They are seeking a more lenient approach to ensure continued investment in state bonds without the added financial burden. The outcome of this request remains to be seen as discussions with the regulator continue.

data.title: China Opposes U.S. Control Over Venezuelan President, Foreign Ministry Says
data.description: On March 25, Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian held a regular press conference. According to Jin10, Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro and his wife are scheduled to appear in a U.S. federal court on March 26. A reporter from Venezuela's Southern Television inquired about the measures China is taking with the international community to ensure the release of the Maduro couple. Lin Jian stated that the U.S. exerting control over a nation's leader is a clear violation of the United Nations Charter's purposes and principles, as well as international law and basic norms of international relations. China firmly opposes such actions and supports Venezuela in safeguarding its sovereignty, dignity, and legitimate rights.

data.title: U.S. House Committee to Discuss Tokenization and Securities Modernization
data.description:
The U.S. House Financial Services Committee is set to hold a hearing titled "Tokenization and the Future of Securities: Modernizing Capital Markets" today at 22:00 UTC+8. According to Foresight News, Salman Banaei, General Counsel of Plume Network, has been invited to testify as the sole representative of emerging Real World Asset (RWA) projects among five key witnesses. He will join members from major Wall Street entities such as Nasdaq, the Depository Trust & Clearing Corporation (DTCC), and the Securities Industry and Financial Markets Association (SIFMA) in the discussion.

Plume Network has already attracted over 220 tokenization projects, covering nearly 50% of non-stablecoin RWA wallets. The hearing will focus on two critical legislative proposals for 2026, marking the formal inclusion of RWAs into the core agenda of U.S. mainstream regulation and capital markets.

data.title: The House Financial Services Committee will hold a hearing today on tokenization and the future of capital markets.
data.description: Today at 18:00 (UTC+8), the House Financial Services Committee will hold a hearing on tokenization and the future of capital markets. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: China to Play Constructive Role in Middle East Peace Efforts, Says Foreign Ministry
data.description: On March 25, according to Jin10, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian addressed a routine press conference. A reporter inquired about a recent phone call between Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi and Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi, during which Araghchi expressed Iran's hope for China's continued active role in promoting peace and ending conflict. Lin Jian stated that the spillover effects of the Iranian conflict are increasingly impacting peace and stability in the Middle East and globally. An immediate ceasefire is the common expectation of regional countries and the international community. Since the outbreak of the conflict, China has been actively working towards peace and will continue its diplomatic efforts as long as the conflict persists. China aims to play a constructive role in promoting peace and ending the conflict, collaborating with regional countries and the international community to restore peace and stability in the Gulf region at the earliest opportunity.

data.title: Safeheron Introduces AML/KYT Product with Enhanced Risk Identification
data.description: Safeheron has unveiled a new Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Know Your Transaction (KYT) product that incorporates services from leading blockchain analysis firms Chainalysis, Elliptic, and MistTrack. According to NS3.AI, the product employs a multi-dimensional hybrid screening approach to enhance risk identification capabilities. Safeheron highlighted that the solution offers customizable risk controls, visual reports, and AI-driven interpretation to facilitate quicker compliance decisions.

data.title: A whale, after a seven-month hiatus, deposited 51,750 SOL tokens into Binance, incurring a loss of $4.37 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale (HnsXQE...c9iyq) deposited 51,750 SOL tokens, worth $4.75 million, into Binance after being dormant for 7 months. This whale had initially withdrawn 50,000 SOL tokens at a price of $9.12 million and staked them, resulting in a loss of $4.37 million from this transaction.
